definition: The blocker lies at the far end of a long sequential call chain; context-sensitive coverage treats each new (call-context, edge) pair along the chain as novel, building a corpus of intermediate stepping-stone seeds that naive discards once the bare edges are saturated.

authoring_contract.goal: ONE parameterized template.c isolating this cluster's shared mechanism, with exactly ONE compile-time -D knob = the program-feature axis. params.json:parameter MUST name that macro; scan_values sweep it; fuzzers = involved_fuzzers; expected_direction = '<winner> > <loser>'.
authoring_contract.isolate: No partial-match coverage edges unless the mechanism IS a gradient (VP families). Keep the gate the only objective.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook folds caller chain into edge-map index, making the false side of cmsIsMatrixShaper a new (call-context, edge) bucket distinct from the already-covered true side, rewarding retention of non-matrix-shaper profile inputs naive discards.
signature.one_line: context-indexed edge map creates new bucket for false-branch under novel caller chain

analysis.summary_one_line: Reach false side of cmsIsMatrixShaper: a v4+ perceptual/saturation profile whose color-space/class is NOT an RGB matrix-shaper.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same program-side condition (non-matrix-shaper v4+ perceptual/saturation profile); the distinguishing input feature is a profile that drives cmsIsMatrixShaper to FALSE, with the false-side basic-block edge becoming new coverage when indexed by the deep call context (cmsDetectBlackPoint reached via ComputeConversion/DefaultICCintents).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx winner seed (the naive_ctx-stored false-branch seed) carries non-RGB signature bytes ('REB' at offsets 0x10-0x12) and reaches the blocker; the false-branch edge is recorded at a context-specific map slot, so a partial advance toward the non-matrix-shaper class registers as new coverage and is preserved.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive records the cmsIsMatrixShaper edge in a single context-agnostic map slot, so once the matrix-shaper true branch (line 219) is covered it gets no new-coverage credit for inputs edging toward the false side, and stays blocked on the 'RGB' true-branch profile.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the (call-context, edge) pair for the false side of cmsIsMatrixShaper is a distinct coverage bucket; this finer granularity rewards intermediate inputs that naive discards, letting the campaign retain and extend toward the non-matrix-shaper profile.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked side of the branch is the false branch of cmsIsMatrixShaper at cmssamp.c:218.
Taking the true branch calls BlackPointAsDarkerColorant and is the loser-only path; the false branch falls through to lines 222-226 which is winner-only.
cmplog beats naive at this branch, attributed to the I2S technique.
cmplog winner seeds carry version byte 05 and non-RGB color-space signatures 'MCH3' and 'Luv '.
naive loser seeds keep the 'RGB' signature and version 04.
At the signature offsets the winner bytes vary across many color spaces while loser bytes are locked to 'RGB'.
The version byte at offset 0x08 differs: winners include 05 while losers are all 04.
cmplog's I2SRandReplace scans the input for one CMP operand and substitutes the other at matching offsets.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile beats naive at this branch, attributed to the value_profile technique.
value_profile winner seed carries 'RG4' at the signature offsets, a one-byte deviation from 'RGB'.
value_profile's CMP_MAP encodes operand closeness so an input closer to matching adds a new bucket and is preserved.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx beats naive at this branch, attributed to the ctx_coverage technique.
naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the call context into the edge-map index so the same edge is a distinct bucket per call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx stored a winner-resolving false-branch seed carrying a non-RGB 'REB' signature.
The false side of cmsIsMatrixShaper is reached through a deep call chain via ComputeConversion and DefaultICCintents.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where cmsIsMatrixShaper's color-space/version comparison operands are derived at runtime (not byte-mapped from the input) so I2SRandReplace has no input pre-image to substitute, yet cmplog still resolves the false branch significantly faster than naive — that would show the win is not from I2S byte substitution of the signature field.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The exact program logic distinguishing a 'matrix-shaper' from a non-matrix-shaper profile is inferred from the byte-diff of the color-space signature and version fields rather than read directly from cmsIsMatrixShaper source, which is not shown in the prompt.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook registers gAMA-handler edges under each caller-chain hash as a distinct coverage bucket, retaining intermediate corpus entries that bring the fuzzer to the gAMA length comparison; plain per-edge naive never builds this corpus scaffolding and cannot mutate the length off 4.
signature.one_line: call-context indexing retains intermediate corpus steps toward gAMA handler enabling length!=4 branch

analysis.summary_one_line: A gAMA chunk whose declared length field is not exactly 4 takes the invalid-length error branch.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same program-side condition: a gAMA chunk reached with length != 4. The feature axis distinguishing the winner here is not byte-solving but the call-context under which the gAMA handler edge is recorded as new coverage.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 10/10 with avg blocked duration only 0.90h. Its CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so reaching png_handle_gAMA via a distinct call path registers as a new (call-context, edge) bucket, retaining intermediate corpus entries that bring the fuzzer to the gAMA length comparison and let havoc flip the length field off 4.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks 10/10 for the full 24.00h. With bare per-edge buckets it does not retain the context-distinct intermediates that lead to the gAMA length check, so it lacks the corpus scaffolding to mutate the length field to a non-4 value and stays on the false branch.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's context-sensitive coverage (CtxHook installs a running call-context hash folded into the edge-map index) makes (call-context, edge) pairs count as new coverage, growing the corpus along the path to the gAMA length check; naive's plain MaxMapFeedback over bare edges does not.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the gAMA length check whose globally blocked side is the true branch.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:

Globally blocked side: T  (true branch)

evidence_trail.claim: The gAMA fourcc sits at input offset 0x25-0x28, so the preceding 4 bytes are the chunk length field.
Winner inputs carry non-zero length bytes at offset 0x23 where losers are uniformly zero, forcing length != 4.
cmplog resolves the true branch in every trial while naive blocks in every trial.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: The exact input offset of the length field is inferred from the gAMA fourcc position and the concentrated byte-diff at 0x23; the harness may carry leading offsets that shift the true length-field offset slightly.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook makes the chunk-dispatch edge into png_handle_sRGB new coverage per distinct call context, preserving intermediate seeds that progress toward the sRGB chunk; bare-edge naive discards re-traversals and never retains the stepping stones to reach length!=1.
signature.one_line: call-context edge coverage preserves chunk-dispatch stepping stones toward sRGB length!=1 branch

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must carry the literal 'sRGB' chunk-type FOURCC (so png_handle_sRGB is reached) with a chunk-length field not equal to 1.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same program-side target — an 'sRGB'-typed chunk reaching png_handle_sRGB with chunk length != 1 — but reached incrementally as the input progresses through the chunk-dispatch call path.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so reaching png_handle_sRGB through a new call context registers as new coverage and the intermediate seed is retained, letting the campaign build toward the length!=1 true side (winner resolved 10/10, branch hit 11 times in W).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: plain naive records only bare edges, so a deeper chunk-dispatch path that re-hits already-covered edges yields no new coverage and the progressing seed is discarded; naive never retains the stepping stones and stays blocked (resolved 1/10, blocked 9/10, avg 21.80h blocked).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook makes (call-context, edge) pairs the coverage unit, so the same chunk-dispatch edge counts as new when reached via a fresh caller chain into png_handle_sRGB; this finer feedback preserves intermediate inputs that drive toward the length!=1 branch, which bare-edge naive cannot.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, making the tIME-dispatch edge under its caller chain a fresh (call-context, edge) bucket that plain naive's bare map already marks covered.

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must contain a tIME chunk whose 4-byte type fourcc dispatches png_handle_tIME with a declared length other than 7

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Reaching the tIME-handling path (line 2490 true branch) via a particular call context, where the same edge counted under its caller chain registers as new coverage even though plain naive already saw the bare edge elsewhere.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 10/10 and the per-role overlay shows the true side at lines 2491-2495 fired as winner-only [W]; its CtxHook folds the call context into the edge-map index so the tIME-dispatch edge under png_handle_tIME's caller chain is a new (call-context, edge) bucket, giving energy to inputs progressing toward the chunk handler.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Plain naive blocks the branch 8/10 (T=0 F=36) and spends 19.50h blocked; its bare per-edge MaxMapFeedback does not distinguish the tIME-dispatch edge by call context, so reaching png_handle_tIME with a non-7-length chunk earns no new bucket and the path is not rewarded.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's context-sensitive coverage (CtxHook running call-context hash folded into the edge-map slot) makes the (call-context, edge) pair leading into the tIME handler count as new coverage, rescuing the input that plain naive's context-blind edge map treats as already-covered.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook folds caller chain into edge-map index so each (call-context, edge) pair along the deep GPOS shaping call chain counts as new coverage, rewarding inputs that progress deeper where bare-edge coverage saturates.
signature.one_line: context-indexed edge map rewards deeper GPOS shaping call paths

analysis.summary_one_line: Input must be a font whose GPOS table sets HB_BUFFER_SCRATCH_FLAG_HAS_GPOS_ATTACHMENT, i.e. drives shaping deep enough to produce a glyph attachment.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The same GPOS-attachment font; the winning edge is that the call-context-indexed coverage map treats reaching the GPOS/shaping functions via new call paths as new coverage, rewarding inputs that drive shaping deeper.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 8/10 and hits the branch 166 times; its CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so each (call-context, edge) pair behind the deep shaping call chain (hops 2-8 in/around position_finish_offsets) counts as new coverage and keeps inputs that progress through it.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks 8/10 (15 hits) because its bare edge-bucket signal saturates on the shared shaping edges and gives no extra reward for reaching them through new call contexts, so it stalls before the attachment flag is set.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's context-sensitive coverage (CtxHook (call-context, edge) buckets via MaxMapFeedback) gives finer reward along the deep GPOS call chain, an advantage naive's context-free edge map lacks.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true branch is a GPOS-attachment scratch-flag test, a deep semantic state rather than a single CMP magic value.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook records (call-context, edge) pairs along the recursive hb_shape_full -> verify -> buffer_verify_unsafe_to_break path, retaining intermediate inputs that naive collapses to already-seen bare edges and discards.
signature.one_line: context-sensitive coverage retains seeds through deep recursive shaping-verify path

analysis.summary_one_line: A well-formed font that shapes a multi-glyph buffer where some glyph carries HB_GLYPH_FLAG_UNSAFE_TO_BREAK so the line-124 mask test goes true.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Same program-side requirement: a valid font shaping a multi-glyph buffer with an UNSAFE_TO_BREAK glyph that makes line 124's mask test true.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves 8/10 with branch hitcount 2615; its CtxHook folds the call-context into the edge-map index so reaching the line-124/shaping code via the recursive hb_shape_full -> verify -> buffer_verify_unsafe_to_break path counts as new (call-context, edge) coverage, retaining intermediate inputs that drive deeper into the verify machinery.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: plain naive collapses these distinct call paths onto the same bare edges, blocks 8/10, and never sets the mask true (line 124 T=0 F=180); its loser seeds are short and never re-enter the shaping path with the context diversity that would expose the UNSAFE_TO_BREAK glyph state.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's context-sensitive coverage (CtxHook (call-context, edge) pairs) preserves inputs that reach the deep recursive shaping/verify path, giving extra corpus retention that naive's bare-edge feedback lacks, so the winner reaches the line-124 true side.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input that drives the layout machinery deep enough (matcher/skipping-iterator/ChainContext dispatch) that the constructor at table_index==1 with num_coords is reached on a call path distinct from shallow parsing.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx records (call-context, edge) pairs as new coverage; the same downstream functions (matcher_t setters, skipping_iterator_t, ChainContext::dispatch) reached via the GPOS-apply call path register as fresh coverage, rewarding the deeper exploration the blocker needs.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive sees only bare edges, so revisiting matcher/iterator code via a deeper call path yields no new coverage and the seeds that would reach the table_index==1 constructor are not retained.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so reaching the layout-apply functions through a new call context counts as new coverage and steers corpus toward the deeper GPOS-apply path.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating on the same conjunction (table_index==1 && num_coords) in which cmplog does NOT outperform naive when the GPOS table tag is the only blind-search barrier — i.e. removing the tag-CMP barrier closes the cmplog>naive gap, showing the divergence was not I2S tag-substitution.

analysis.weakest_evidence_point: Attributing the cmplog win specifically to GPOS-tag I2S substitution rests on the tag bytes appearing in winner seeds plus a 2-row dash floor; the dash rows confirm SOME I2S find but not that it planted the table tag rather than another header constant.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook indexes the kern-decision edges by caller chain so the same branch in compile() is a fresh (call-context, edge) slot per unique call path, preserving intermediate font-state inputs that naive discards once the bare edge is bucketed.
signature.one_line: context-indexed kern-decision edge keeps varied font-state inputs alive

analysis.summary_one_line: Font must carry a structurally valid kern table that reaches has_machine_kerning returning false, not all-0x20 padding.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A font that reaches the kern-table path through the same edges naive hits but under a call context that registers as new coverage, sustaining corpus entries that drive deeper into hb_ot_shape_planner_t::compile toward the blocker.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx folds the caller chain (compile <- init0 <- create2 ...) into the edge-map index, so edges in the kern-decision block at L168-L187 are recorded as new (call-context, edge) pairs, keeping more intermediate font states alive and exploring the false side.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive uses bare edge buckets, so once the L185-L187 edges are hit once under any context they no longer yield new coverage; the loser's 0x20-padded inputs never accrue the structural variety needed and it remains blocked (resolved 2/10).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook indexes edges by call context, turning the same kern-decision edge into multiple distinct coverage slots and thereby preserving inputs that incrementally build the valid kern table that flips the branch false.

signature.mechanism_summary: CtxHook gives each SAX2 call path into xmlSplitQName its own edge-map slot, retaining intermediate seeds that build a longer oversized prefix through entity/attribute processing states that naive prunes as already-covered.
signature.one_line: Context-indexed QName loop edges retain intermediate seeds building oversized prefix

analysis.summary_one_line: A long-prefix QName (len>=XML_MAX_NAMELEN) terminated so the huge-name copy loop at 3013 exits via its false side after a colon

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An XML element/attribute QName whose prefix exceeds XML_MAX_NAMELEN so the dynamic-buffer copy loop at parser.c:3013 is entered, and which is terminated (by 0 or ':') so the loop takes its false exit rather than spinning forever on more name bytes; this is reached only through deep, well-formed enough SAX element/attribute parsing (entity refs, references, attribute processing).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx seeds carry a recognizable XML skeleton (`\n<ml v...`, `<?xml version=`, `<ISO-10646-UCS-2`) and runs of repeated bytes (0xc0, 0xb2, 0xb5, 'x') that form an oversized name; the winner's hit-count profile shows it drives the deeper parser states the false-exit path requires (xmlParseEntityRef W=72 vs L=0, xmlParseReference W=54 vs L=0), and the blocker's false side is actually taken in the W role (L3013 F=36/F=6 with buffer present).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive seeds are dominated by '.' fill (0x2e) and a `\n<Rxul...` skeleton that never builds an oversized terminated prefix and never reaches the entity/reference parsing depth (xmlParseEntityRef/xmlParseReference L hits = 0); the loser blocks the false side because the surrounding parse states are never explored, not because of any single comparand byte.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers share the identical havoc+token mutator stack with no I2S and no CMP_MAP, so the divergence is not comparison-solving. naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller call-context hash into the edge-map index, so an edge inside xmlSplitQName reached via a NEW SAX2 call path (e.g. through xmlSAX2AttributeInternal vs xmlSAX2StartElement) registers as new coverage. That extra granularity rewards and retains the intermediate seeds that progressively build a longer prefix and deeper parse state, letting the context-sensitive arm climb to the loop's false-exit state that the bare-edge naive arm prunes as already-covered.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is naive_ctx > naive with delta ctx_coverage, the only divergence being coverage granularity.
The blocker is the false (loop-exit) side of the huge-name copy loop at parser.c:3013 inside xmlSplitQName.

analysis.summary_one_line: Reaching the SAX-guard FALSE side in a big-buffer char-data flush, which requires disableSAX==1 reached via a distinct error-then-content call context.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An input that drives the parser into xmlParseCharDataComplex with a >=XML_PARSER_BIG_BUFFER_SIZE character run while ctxt->disableSAX is true (a prior fatal error has set disableSAX==1), so the guard (sax!=NULL && !disableSAX) at L4653 evaluates FALSE; the well-formed DOCTYPE-bearing XML of the winner seeds reaches this state through a richer set of caller call-contexts than the malformed loser inputs.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds are well-formed XML beginning '<?xml version="1.0"?>' with a '<!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/1...' header, exercising clean parser states that let naive_ctx's context-indexed feedback retain many distinct (call-context, edge) corpus entries reaching the blocker; the divergent-branch dump shows the winner does take the FALSE side (W L4653 T=0 F=2) which the loser never reaches.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are malformed byte soup (e.g. 'ht~p://-/' and long runs of repeated bytes) that stall on the TRUE side only (L L4653 T=16 F=0); plain naive's bare-edge feedback collapses the many call paths into xmlParseCharDataComplex onto the same edge slots, so it never retains a corpus path that arrives at the big-buffer flush with disableSAX==1.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx and naive share the identical havoc+token mutator stack with no I2S or CMP_MAP; the only differing technique is naive_ctx's CtxHook, which folds the caller-chain hash into the edge-map index so the same blocker edge counts as new coverage when reached through a new call context. This context granularity preserves more diverse paths into xmlParseCharDataComplex, eventually surfacing an input whose disableSAX state flips the L4653 guard FALSE.

hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx folds the call-context hash into the edge-map index, so the same xmlParseMarkupDecl switch edges register as distinct (call-context, edge) pairs across the internal-subset / external-subset / conditional-section call paths; this rewards exploring the markup-decl switch arms and drives the corpus toward inputs that exercise case '-'.

signature.mechanism_summary: Context-sensitive coverage gives a fresh bucket to xmlSAX2ProcessingInstruction reached via the internal-DTD-subset parse path, retaining PI-inside-subset seeds that bare naive collapses with the much more common root-level PI edge.
signature.one_line: PI inside DTD internal subset (inSubset==1); ctx separates subset call-context edge

analysis.summary_one_line: A processing instruction (<?...?>) must appear inside an internal DTD subset so ctxt->inSubset==1 holds at the PI handler.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The input must contain a processing instruction emitted while the parser is inside the internal DTD subset, i.e. a `<?...?>` between `<!DOCTYPE name [` and the closing `]>`, so that ctxt->inSubset == 1 when xmlSAX2ProcessingInstruction runs and the true branch at L2740 is taken (then xmlAddChild to intSubset at L2741).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: The naive_ctx winner resolves 8/10 and takes the true side (L2740 T=2 in W vs T=0 in L); its seed carries a well-formed `<?xml version="1.0"?>` prolog plus a `<!DOCTYPE a...` declaration, the structural scaffold needed to enter inSubset==1 when a PI is parsed inside the subset. Context-sensitive coverage rewards the PI handler reached via the DTD-internal-subset call context as a distinct new edge, so the path into the subset PI handler stays in the corpus and gets driven to the inSubset==1 site.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks 10/10 and only ever takes the false side (L2740 T=0 F=91 in L); it reaches xmlSAX2ProcessingInstruction 91 times but with inSubset==0, so it falls through to the parent/root handling at L2747-L2755 (the [L] lines). Its inputs have the DOCTYPE/PI fragments mangled and never assemble a PI nested inside an open internal subset, so the inSubset==1 state never co-occurs with a PI parse.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: The only technique delta between naive_ctx and naive is the CtxHook: naive_ctx folds a running call-context hash into the edge-map index, so reaching xmlSAX2ProcessingInstruction via the internal-subset parse path is a fresh (call-context, edge) coverage bucket that naive's bare-edge map cannot distinguish from the much more frequent root-level PI path. This context-sensitive coverage keeps the subset-PI input alive in the corpus and steers mutation toward the inSubset==1 true branch; the mutators are identical havoc/token (no I2S, no CMP_MAP).

signature.mechanism_summary: Context-sensitive coverage rewards the xmlDtdDumpOutput edge reached via the XML_DTD_NODE dispatch as a new (call-context, edge) bucket, providing stepping-stone retention for seeds that populate DTD member tables that bare-edge naive collapses to covered.
signature.one_line: Non-empty DTD in xmlDtdDumpOutput; ctx retains DTD-population path via caller-context edge

analysis.summary_one_line: DTD with a non-empty subset body (at least one of entities/elements/attributes/notations/pentities populated), forcing the ' [' content path.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An XML input whose <!DOCTYPE ...> internal subset declares at least one DTD member (element decl, attribute decl, entity, notation, or parameter entity), so dtd->elements (etc.) is non-NULL and the line-686 conjunction is false, taking the ' [\n' body-dump branch rather than the bare '>' close.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx winning seeds carry DOCTYPE-with-content structure: seed 2/3 contain '<!DOCTYPE a S[<!ELEMENT' and the W role uniquely executes xmlBufDumpElementDecl (W=10, L=0), i.e. an element declaration was parsed into dtd->elements; on the blocker the winner takes the false (body) side (L686 W: T=0 F=10) and proceeds into the children loop (L706 W: T=12 F=10).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive loser seeds reach the DTD node but stay on the empty-DTD true side (L686 L: T=10 F=0) and never enter the body; the loser never invokes xmlBufDumpElementDecl (L=0) and instead exercises the SYSTEM-id path / escaping functions, meaning its parsed DTD has all five member tables NULL.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers share the identical havoc+token mutator stack with no I2S and no CMP_MAP; the only difference is naive_ctx's CtxHook, which folds the caller chain into the edge-map index so that reaching the line-686 edge via the XML_DTD_NODE dispatch in xmlNodeDumpOutputInternal is recorded as a NEW (call-context, edge) bucket. This context-sensitive coverage rewards the intermediate states along the DTD-population path, giving the corpus the structural stepping-stones to build a non-empty-subset DOCTYPE, whereas bare-edge naive collapses those distinct contexts onto already-covered edges and gets no new-coverage signal to retain them.

signature.mechanism_summary: Context-sensitive coverage retains seeds reaching the 3-byte UTF-8 decode block via new caller chains; the extra granularity gives energy to refine bytes toward the overlong-3-byte case (decoded val < 0x800) that bare-edge feedback cannot distinguish.
signature.one_line: Overlong 3-byte UTF-8 val < 0x800; ctx retains seeds via new caller-chain buckets

analysis.summary_one_line: A 3-byte UTF-8 sequence (lead 0xE0-0xEF + two 0x80-0xBF continuations) decoding to an overlong value < 0x800

hypotheses.what_input_feature: The input must reach xmlCurrentChar with a 3-byte UTF-8 lead byte (c & 0xf0 == 0xe0) followed by two valid continuation bytes (each (b & 0xc0)==0x80) such that the decoded value (cur0&0xf)<<12 | (cur1&0x3f)<<6 | (cur2&0x3f) is below 0x800 — i.e. an overlong 3-byte encoding that triggers the goto encoding_error true side at line 622.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx resolves the branch in 8/10 trials and actually executes the true side (W shows T=42 F=0 at line 622), reaching the 3-byte decode block (lines 617-624 marked [B] in the per-role overlay). Context-sensitive coverage indexes the same xmlCurrentChar edge by its distinct call context (e.g. the xmlParseCharDataComplex -> xmlCurrentChar path), so an input that lands a 3-byte sequence in a never-before-seen caller chain registers as new coverage and is retained, letting energy accumulate on the overlong-3-byte case.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive blocks the branch in 8/10 trials and never takes the true side (L shows T=0 F=1730 at line 622): it produces many 3-byte sequences but they all decode to >= 0x800, so they hit the same flat edge and never get extra retention. Without context indexing the overlong-3-byte input is not distinguished from the common valid-3-byte input, so naive lacks the corpus pressure to refine bytes toward val < 0x800.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller chain into the edge-map index, so the xmlCurrentChar 3-byte decode edge counts as new coverage when reached via a new call context; this extra granularity retains intermediate seeds that naive's bare-edge feedback discards, giving the schedule the foothold to drive the decoded value below 0x800. This is context-sensitive coverage, not comparison-solving.
